stories.title: MAP paper spec + metrics harness
stories.description:
**MAP paper spec + metrics harness**

- Files to touch: `docs/specs/map-paper-spec.md`, `src/eval/map_harness.py`, `tests/test_map_harness.py`
- Distill MAP (Nature Communications 2025, s41467-025-63804-5) into a spec: 5 modules (conflict monitor, state predictor, evaluator, decomposer, coordinator), inputs/outputs, canonical benchmarks.
- Build a metric harness that runs the same cognitive benchmarks the MAP paper uses (or closest public equivalents) and emits a JSON report with per-module scores.
- Acceptance: spec committed; `pytest tests/test_map_harness.py` passes; harness runs end-to-end on a mock agent and produces a valid JSON report.
- Dependencies: none.

stories.title: Benchmark v0.2 Dispatcher vs MAP conflict monitor
stories.description:
**Benchmark v0.2 Dispatcher vs MAP conflict monitor**

- Files to touch: `src/eval/map_dispatcher_bench.py`, `results/map-dispatcher.json`
- Run MAP's conflict-monitor benchmark against v0.2's Dispatcher (7 meta-intents, training-free YAML mapping).
- Score: agreement rate with MAP reference conflict labels; false-positive rate; latency.
- Acceptance: script runs, emits `results/map-dispatcher.json` with ≥ 3 metrics. Descriptive, not pass/fail.
- Dependencies: step 1.

stories.title: Benchmark v0.2 Negotiator vs MAP state evaluator
stories.description:
**Benchmark v0.2 Negotiator vs MAP state evaluator**

- Files to touch: `src/eval/map_negotiator_bench.py`, `results/map-negotiator.json`
- Run MAP's state-evaluation benchmark against v0.2's Negotiator (CAMP arbitration + Catfish dissent, adaptive judge).
- Score: Spearman correlation with MAP reference scores, escalation rate, judge cost.
- Acceptance: emits `results/map-negotiator.json`. Goal is structural mapping, not supremacy.
- Dependencies: step 1.

stories.title: Technical report: v0.2 as MAP implementation
stories.description:
**Technical report: v0.2 as MAP implementation**

- Files to touch: `docs/specs/map-validation-report.md`
- Retrospective technical note: for each of MAP's 5 modules, identify the v0.2 component that implements the same function (Dispatcher ↔ conflict monitor, Negotiator ↔ state evaluator + coordinator, Aeon ↔ state predictor memory, etc.).
- Include numeric results from steps 2-3.
- Acceptance: report committed, ~500-800 lines, references steps 2-3 results, no unresolved TODOs.
- Dependencies: steps 2, 3.

stories.title: AeonSleep architecture spec
stories.description:
**AeonSleep architecture spec**

- Files to touch: `docs/specs/aeonsleep-architecture.md`
- Design doc for the unified module: spatial (Atlas SIMD), episodic (Trace graph), temporal consolidation (SleepGate conflict-aware tagger), forgetting gate (learned MLP selective eviction), consolidation module (summarization-based episode merge).
- Public API: `AeonSleep.write(episode)`, `.recall(query)`, `.sleep_cycle()`, `.query_time(range)`, `.stats()`.
- Include migration mapping from v0.2 Aeon (what code transfers, what gets replaced, what's new).
- Acceptance: spec committed, all 5 public methods documented with pre/post conditions, data-flow diagram included.
- Dependencies: none.

stories.title: Port Atlas SIMD index from v0.2
stories.description:
**Port Atlas SIMD index from v0.2**

- Files to touch: `src/memory/atlas.py`, `tests/test_atlas.py`
- Implement the SIMD vector index per v0.2 spec (`docs/specs/2026-04-15-cognitive-layer-design.md`). Pure classical hash-based vector search.
- Acceptance: `pytest tests/test_atlas.py::test_roundtrip` passes (write 1000 vectors, recall top-10 by cosine, latency < 5 ms on Mac Studio).
- Dependencies: step 5.

stories.title: Port Trace neuro-symbolic graph from v0.2
stories.description:
**Port Trace neuro-symbolic graph from v0.2**

- Files to touch: `src/memory/trace.py`, `tests/test_trace.py`
- Implement the Aeon Trace graph: episode nodes + typed edges (temporal, causal, topical), NetworkX backend.
- Acceptance: `pytest tests/test_trace.py` passes — create episode chain, query ancestors, verify graph invariants.
- Dependencies: step 5.

stories.title: SleepGate conflict-aware temporal tagger
stories.description:
**SleepGate conflict-aware temporal tagger**

- Files to touch: `src/cognitive/sleep_tagger.py`, `tests/test_sleep_tagger.py`
- Implement the conflict tagger from arxiv 2603.14517: for each new episode, compute a conflict score vs recent episodes; tag `conflict_level ∈ [0,1]` and `reason` (topic, contradiction, stale).
- Scorer uses small sentence-transformer similarity + rule-based logic (no LLM call in hot path).
- Acceptance: `pytest tests/test_sleep_tagger.py` passes on synthetic planted conflicts; precision ≥ 0.8, recall ≥ 0.7.
- Dependencies: step 7.

stories.title: Forgetting gate network
stories.description:
**Forgetting gate network**

- Files to touch: `src/cognitive/forgetting_gate.py`, `scripts/train_forgetting_gate.py`, `data/forgetting-pairs.jsonl`, `tests/test_forgetting_gate.py`
- Small MLP (2 hidden layers) taking features (age, access count, conflict level, embedding norm) → P(keep).
- Train on 2k synthetic PI-style pairs.
- Acceptance: test set F1 ≥ 0.85; training reproducible from script.
- Dependencies: step 8.

stories.title: Consolidation module
stories.description:
**Consolidation module**

- Files to touch: `src/cognitive/consolidation.py`, `tests/test_consolidation.py`
- Cluster similar episodes by topic + temporal proximity, summarize each cluster via teacher LLM into a single consolidated episode, preserve backrefs (originals not destroyed).
- Use Qwen3.5-35B-A3B (kxkm-ai :8000 via tunnel) for summarization.
- Acceptance: 100 episodes across 10 topics → ≤ 20 summary nodes; recall via summary node ≥ 0.9 on held-out QA probes.
- Dependencies: steps 7, 8.

stories.title: Unified AeonSleep API
stories.description:
**Unified AeonSleep API**

- Files to touch: `src/memory/aeonsleep.py`, `src/memory/__init__.py`, `tests/test_aeonsleep.py`
- Single entry-point class wrapping Atlas, Trace, SleepTagger, ForgettingGate, Consolidation.
- Methods: `write`, `recall(query, k=10)`, `sleep_cycle()`, `query_time(range)`, `stats()`.
- Acceptance: integration test — write 500 episodes, run 3 sleep cycles, achieve **≥ 95% retrieval accuracy at PI depth 10** on SleepGate-paper benchmark.
- Dependencies: steps 6, 7, 8, 9, 10.

stories.title: SpikingBrain-76B acquisition plan
stories.description:
**SpikingBrain-76B acquisition plan**

- Files to touch: `docs/specs/spikingbrain-acquisition.md`, `scripts/probe_spikingbrain_hf.py`
- Probe HuggingFace for an official or community SpikingBrain-76B checkpoint (BICLab, paper authors, arxiv-linked repos).
- Three paths: (a) official checkpoint → download; (b) community checkpoint → verify license + provenance; (c) no checkpoint → fallback: Spikingformer training-free conversion of Qwen2.5-7B as SpikingBrain-7B.
- Acceptance: spec committed; probe script emits `results/spikingbrain-probe.json` with decision.
- Dependencies: none.

stories.title: Studio environment setup (PyTorch path)
stories.description:
**Studio environment setup (PyTorch path)**

- Files to touch: `pyproject.toml` (optional `neuro` extra), `scripts/setup_neuro_env.sh`, `docs/setup-studio-neuro.md`
- SpikingBrain + Spikingformer are PyTorch-first. MLX not supported. Document MPS vs CPU tradeoff on Studio M3 Ultra.
- Add optional extra `neuro` pulling `spikingjelly`, `torch>=2.5`, `transformers`, `accelerate`.
- Acceptance: `uv sync --extra neuro` succeeds; `python -c "import spikingjelly; import torch; print(torch.backends.mps.is_available())"` prints `True`.
- Dependencies: step 12.

stories.title: Smoke inference on BF16 SpikingBrain checkpoint
stories.description:
**Smoke inference on BF16 SpikingBrain checkpoint**

- Files to touch: `scripts/smoke_spikingbrain.py`, `results/spikingbrain-smoke.json`
- Load SpikingBrain-76B (or 7B fallback per step 12) in BF16, run prompt `hello, what are you?`, verify non-empty non-garbage output.
- Measure peak unified-memory footprint; target ≤ 480 GB BF16 for 76B, ≤ 20 GB for 7B fallback.
- Acceptance: smoke JSON contains `{prompt, response, peak_mem_gb, tokens_s}`; response ≥ 20 chars.
- Dependencies: step 13.

stories.title: Q4 quantization of SpikingBrain
stories.description:
**Q4 quantization of SpikingBrain**

- Files to touch: `scripts/quantize_spikingbrain.py`, `models/spikingbrain-q4.gguf` (gitignored), `docs/specs/spikingbrain-quant.md`
- Try llama.cpp + Q4_K_M. If SNN layers break op set (expected), fall back to `bitsandbytes` 4-bit inside PyTorch (no GGUF).
- Target: ≥ 10 tok/s on quantized model on Studio.
- Acceptance: `results/spikingbrain-quant.json` with `{quant_method, size_gb, tokens_s}`; if llama.cpp fails, spec documents error + fallback result.
- Dependencies: step 14.

stories.title: SpikingBrain-76B architecture spec
stories.description:
**SpikingBrain-76B architecture spec**

- Files to touch: `docs/specs/spikingbrain-76b.md`
- Technical reference: layer structure, spiking neuron type (LIF/PLIF), MoE routing mechanism (differs from Qwen MoE), integration points for SNN conversion, known gotchas from released code.
- Acceptance: spec committed, ≥ 300 lines, cites arxiv 2509.05276 and code commits examined.
- Dependencies: steps 14, 15.

stories.title: Spikingformer library integration
stories.description:
**Spikingformer library integration**

- Files to touch: `src/spiking/__init__.py`, `src/spiking/formatter.py`, `tests/test_spikingformer.py`
- Integrate Spikingformer (AAAI 2026) for training-free ANN → SNN conversion.
- Test on Qwen2-0.5B first to verify pipeline before touching 76B.
- Acceptance: conversion of Qwen2-0.5B preserves ≥ 95% base accuracy on HellaSwag subset (100 samples); activation sparsity ≥ 70%.
- Dependencies: step 13.

stories.title: Training-free conversion of SpikingBrain layer subset
stories.description:
**Training-free conversion of SpikingBrain layer subset**

- Files to touch: `scripts/convert_spikingbrain_subset.py`, `results/spikingbrain-snn-convert.json`
- Convert MoE router + 2-3 attention heads of SpikingBrain from ANN-style to full SNN via Spikingformer.
- Target: match dense baseline PPL within ±5% on 1K held-out tokens.
- Acceptance: convert JSON with PPL delta, spike rate, layer map; delta ≤ 5%.
- Dependencies: steps 16, 17.

stories.title: Energy benchmark (theoretical + measured)
stories.description:
**Energy benchmark (theoretical + measured)**

- Files to touch: `scripts/energy_bench.py`, `results/energy-bench.json`, `docs/specs/energy-methodology.md`
- Theoretical: FLOPs for dense inference vs spike operations for converted subset.
- Measured: if Akida hw available (step 22+) run on hw; otherwise simulator-only.
- Acceptance: JSON with theoretical FLOPs→spikes ratio AND measured/simulated watt-hour; methodology doc committed.
- Dependencies: step 18.

stories.title: Loihi 2 simulator setup
stories.description:
**Loihi 2 simulator setup**

- Files to touch: `scripts/setup_loihi2_sim.sh`, `docs/setup-loihi.md`, `tests/test_loihi_sim_smoke.py`
- Install Intel KAPOHO SDK or `lava-nc` open-source fallback.
- Run a `hello spike` example to validate install.
- Acceptance: `pytest tests/test_loihi_sim_smoke.py::test_blink` passes — drives canonical spike pattern through simulator.
- Dependencies: step 17.

stories.title: BrainChip Akida simulator setup
stories.description:
**BrainChip Akida simulator setup**

- Files to touch: `scripts/setup_akida_sim.sh`, `docs/setup-akida.md`, `tests/test_akida_sim_smoke.py`
- Install Akida SDK (pip `akida`) + MetaTF + quantization toolkit.
- Run reference MNIST/CIFAR example. Deploy step-18 MoE router on simulator.
- Acceptance: reference passes AND MoE router simulator routing matches Spikingformer baseline ±2% accuracy.
- Dependencies: step 18.

stories.title: Order Akida Mini PCIe + driver setup on kxkm-ai
stories.description:
**Order Akida Mini PCIe + driver setup on kxkm-ai**

- Files to touch: `docs/hardware/akida-pcie-setup.md`, `scripts/akida_pcie_probe.py`
- **Cost gate**: only proceed if step 21 simulator is green.
- Order BrainChip Akida Mini PCIe (~$300). Install in kxkm-ai. Install Linux drivers. Verify with `akida devices` CLI.
- Acceptance: probe script runs on kxkm-ai, prints card info (firmware, cores, mem), enrolls into SDK.
- Dependencies: step 21. **Budget: $300 one-time.**

stories.title: Deploy SpikingBrain subset on Akida physical
stories.description:
**Deploy SpikingBrain subset on Akida physical**

- Files to touch: `scripts/deploy_akida_physical.py`, `results/akida-deploy.json`
- Flash step-21 simulator-validated MoE router subset to physical Akida Mini PCIe.
- Measure: wall-clock latency, watt draw, throughput (tokens/s for routing decision).
- Acceptance: latency ≤ 10 ms per routing decision, watt draw logged, agreement with simulator ≥ 98%.
- Dependencies: step 22.

stories.title: STRETCH — ESP32-S3 custom SNN port
stories.description:
**STRETCH — ESP32-S3 custom SNN port**

- Files to touch: `firmware/esp32-snn/` (new), `docs/specs/esp32-snn-port.md`
- **OPTIONAL, marked stretch goal.** Port minimal SNN inference kernel to ESP32-S3 Xtensa. Base on Zacus firmware tooling.
- Scope: one small spiking layer (64 neurons LIF) on pre-computed input pattern.
- Expected effort: 2-3 weeks Xtensa dev. Skip if time-boxed.
- Acceptance (if attempted): `idf.py build` succeeds, firmware prints spike output on UART, energy logged via INA probe if available.
- Dependencies: none hard. **OPTIONAL — v0.3 ships without this if unfinished.**

stories.title: End-to-end acceptance test
stories.description:
**End-to-end acceptance test**

- Files to touch: `tests/test_e2e_neuro.py`, `scripts/run_e2e_neuro.py`, `results/e2e-neuro.json`
- Integration: AeonSleep + SpikingBrain-76B (or 7B fallback) + Akida routing.
- Canonical scenario: inject 200 planted memories, run 1 sleep cycle, recall prompt, route through SpikingBrain with Akida-assisted MoE routing; check response non-empty + coherent.
- Acceptance targets: AeonSleep PI-depth-10 accuracy ≥ 95%, SpikingBrain latency ≤ 10 s/token on Studio Q4, Akida agreement with baseline ≥ 98%. All three must pass.
- Dependencies: steps 11, 14, 23.

stories.title: HuggingFace release + model card + cookbook
stories.description:
**HuggingFace release + model card + cookbook**

- Files to touch: `docs/release-v0.3.md`, `docs/cookbook-v0.3.md`, `MODEL_CARD-v0.3.md`
- Publish as `electron-rare/micro-kiki-v0.3-neuroscience`: SpikingBrain weights, AeonSleep code, model card declaring caveats (research-grade, 2026 preprint-based, hardware requirement for Akida).
- Cookbook: 3 worked examples — (a) AeonSleep standalone, (b) SpikingBrain-7B CPU inference, (c) Akida-accelerated routing.
- Acceptance: HF repo exists + downloadable; model card has standard sections; cookbook notebooks execute end-to-end on fresh Studio env.
- Dependencies: step 25.
